case_name: Raouf Hanna, DDS and Hanna Dental Implants, LLLP v. Thomas Neil Turner

summary.brief: Raouf Hanna, DDS and Hanna Dental Implants, LLLP v. Thomas Neil Turner. Medical malpractice case involving neurology.

evidence_index.text: Turner alleges that Hanna assured him he was a good candidate for dental implants after performing a single, limited evaluation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Hanna extracted all of Turner’s remaining teeth, 27 in total, and installed several implants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Turner reported that his lip sometimes tingled and some liquids ran out of his nose when he drank.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Turner learned for the first time that dental screws implanted in his jaw by Hanna were protruding into his nasal passageway and his left maxillary sinus.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Rosenfeld concludes that Hanna violated the standard of care in several ways.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Rosenfeld opines that the standard of care required Hanna to use full-mouth periapical x-rays and three-dimensional imaging.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Rosenfeld further opines that the standard of care required Hanna to vigilantly monitor Turner for nasal and sinus perforations.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: We hold that the trial court did not abuse its discretion in concluding that Rosenfeld’s supplemental expert report is adequate with respect to causation.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: Rosenfeld’s expert report is adequate as to the standard of care and its breach.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Turner has continued to experience problems afterward and has required additional treatment for a reoccurring sinus infection and sinus inflammation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Desire for a permanent solution to dental issues
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Turner sought treatment for past dental work issues and was assured by Hanna that he was a good candidate for dental implants after a limited evaluation.

focused_exam.oral: Limited evaluation performed, single panoramic x-ray taken

requestables.name: Full-mouth periapical x-rays
requestables.description: X-rays that image the entire tooth and supporting bone

reveal.clinical_significance: Would have assessed jawbone integrity and implant suitability
reveal.decision_impact: Failure to perform contributed to complications

requestables.name: Three-dimensional imaging
requestables.description: Imaging to assess bone structure and implant placement

reveal.clinical_significance: Would have identified complications such as nasal and sinus perforations
reveal.decision_impact: Failure to perform led to undiagnosed complications

timeline_phases.name: Initial Evaluation and Planning
timeline_phases.description: Patient evaluated for dental implant candidacy

key_events.event: Limited evaluation and single panoramic x-ray performed

timeline_phases.name: Dental Implant Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Extraction of teeth and placement of implants

key_events.event: Extraction of 27 teeth and placement of 9 implants
Patient reports symptoms, reassured by provider

key_events.timestamp: Just over three years after initial procedure

decision_points.clinical_context: Initial evaluation for dental implant candidacy
decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic imaging should be performed before proceeding with dental implants?

options.description: Order full-mouth periapical x-rays and three-dimensional imaging
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires comprehensive imaging to assess bone integrity and implant suitability

options.description: Proceed with single panoramic x-ray only
options.clinical_reasoning: Insufficient for assessing detailed bone structure and potential complications

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant performed a single panoramic x-ray

expected_action_court.description: Full-mouth periapical x-rays and three-dimensional imaging should have been ordered
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established comprehensive imaging as standard

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform comprehensive imaging led to undiagnosed complications
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Comprehensive imaging would have identified bone deficiencies and potential complications

decision_points.prompt: How should the patient be monitored for complications after implant placement?

options.description: Vigilantly monitor for nasal and sinus perforations and nerve injuries
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard of care requires monitoring for complications to prevent permanent damage

options.description: Reassure patient and attribute symptoms to normal healing
options.clinical_reasoning: Failed to address and investigate reported symptoms

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant reassured patient without further investigation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Symptoms attributed to normal healing process

expected_action_court.description: Patient should have been monitored for complications
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for vigilant monitoring

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to monitor led to prolonged and exacerbated injuries
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Monitoring would have allowed for timely intervention

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed nasal and sinus perforations, nerve damage, and required removal of implants
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Chronic sinus infections, impaired chewing, permanent nerve damage

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to perform comprehensive imaging
Causation established - earlier intervention would have prevented complications

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to perform comprehensive imaging and monitoring
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Comprehensive imaging and monitoring would have identified and addressed complications

factual_timeline.event: Initial evaluation with limited assessment and single panoramic x-ray
Extraction of 27 teeth and placement of 9 implants
Patient reports symptoms, reassured by provider

factual_timeline.timestamp: Just over three years after initial procedure

diagnoses.diagnosis: Nasal and sinus perforations, nerve damage
diagnoses.when_made: Just over three years after initial procedure

complications.complication: Nasal and sinus perforations, nerve damage

allegations.allegation: Failure to perform comprehensive imaging and monitoring
